State: Alaska
No Tax
Economic Nexus Rules: Although there is no state-level sales tax in Alaska and no state-wide economic nexus rules, a portion of local governments in Alaska have signed an intergovernmental agreement establishing a Commission that will provide governance over a streamlined, single-level administration of sales tax collection and remittance. Locals that are members of the Commission and have adopted their uniform sales tax code require remote sellers and marketplace facilitators to collect and remit sales tax into those jurisdictions if their gross statewide sales of property, products or services delivered into the state within the last calendar year meets or exceeds $100,000. As of January 1, 2025, the 200 transaction threshold has been removed. Additional information may also be found on the Commission’s website: https://arsstc.org/
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators are considered the remote seller for each sale facilitated through its marketplace and must collect and remit sales tax if they meet the economic thresholds mentioned above.
Sales Tax Holidays: There are no sales tax holiday at the state level. Several localities have seasonal exemptions or reduced rates. Additionally, locals are may enact their own sales tax holidays.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: There is no state level rule. Certain localities have a maximum taxable amount threshold that can apply either to the total transaction or at each item depending on the local rules.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: There is no state level rule.

State: Hawaii

Economic Nexus Rules: Hawaii imposes a tax collection and remittance obligation is created if a seller has 200 or more transactions or $100,000 or more in sales into the state in the preceding or current calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Hawaii deems marketplace facilitators as the sellers of tangible personal property, intangible property, or services however, marketplace sellers are deemed to be making wholesale sales to the facilitators. Therefore, the state will collect 4% GET from marketplace facilitators and an additional 0.5% from marketplace sellers that meet the Hawaii economic nexus threshold.

State: The Hawaii marketplace law requires other persons who provide a forum for listing of tangible personal property and the taking or processing of orders to report information about purchasers to the Department of Taxation.
Economic Nexus Rules: Yes. Counties are authorized to adopt a surcharge on the 4% GE tax rate. Businesses may pass on the GE tax and any applicable county surcharge to customers at the maximum pass-on rate. Three counties in the state of Hawaii have adopted said surcharge with a current rate of 0.50%; Honolulu, Kauai, and Hawaii. Effective January 1, 2024 Maui will begin imposing a 0.50% surcharge. The maximum pass-on rate on these counties is 4.7120%.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: No

State: Semi-annually when sales tax liability is <= $2,000.00 per year
Quarterly when sales tax liability is <= $4,000.00 per year
Monthly when sales tax liability is> $4,000.00 per year
Monthly: All who do not have a GET license (those with a GET license must file G-45/G-49 instead.)
Independent Local Nexus: Annual: 20th day of the 4th month following the close of the tax year

State: Semi-annual: 20th day of the calendar month following the end of the filing period
Quarter: 20th day of the calendar month following the end of the filing period
Month: 20th day of the calendar month following the end of the filing period
Weekend Rules - File on the next regular work day
Alabama

Independent Local Nexus: Locals have independent nexus. City and county rates are imposed in addition to the state rate. These local rates vary depending on where you have nexus within the state and are subject to change monthly.
Economic Nexus Rules: Persons lacking physical presence in the state are "engaging in business" if their total taxable gross receipts from sales, leases and licenses of tangible personal property, sales of licenses and sales of services and licenses for use of real property sourced to the state exceeded $250,000 in the previous calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect Wyoming sales tax when their gross revenue from sales made or facilitated into Wyoming exceeds $100,000.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes: Severe Weather Preparedness - last full weekend of February; Back to School - third Friday of July through the following Sunday
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually when sales tax liability is $600 or less during the preceding calendar year

State: Quarterly when sales tax liability is less than $1,200/month during the preceding calendar year
Monthly when sales tax liability is greater than $2,400/month during the preceding calendar year

If the 20th falls on a weekend or holiday, the return must be postmarked on or before the due date to be timely filed. EFT: If the date on which the taxpayer is required to initiate an electronic payment transaction falls on a weekend or holiday, the electronic payment information must be transmitted by 4:00 pm (Central Standard Time) on or before the due date, to be considered timely filed.
Arkansas

Economic Nexus Rules: All remote sellers are required to collect and remit Sales and Use tax to the State of Arkansas if within the current or previous year the sale of tangible personal property, taxable services, a digital code, or specified digital products for delivery into Arkansas exceeded one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) or two hundred (200) transactions.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: All marketplace facilitators are required to collect and remit Sales and Use tax to the State of Arkansas if within the current or previous year the sale of tangible personal property, taxable services, a digital code, or specified digital products for delivery into Arkansas exceeded one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) or two hundred (200) transactions.
Sales Tax Holidays: Back to school holiday which typically takes place the first full weekend in August
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: New Businesses are required to be Monthly filers unless authorized by the Director

State: Quarterly - When the average amount of tax for which the taxpayer is liable for the previous fiscal year (July 1 through June 30) does not exceed $100.00 per month, the Director may notify the taxpayer that the taxpayer may file quarterly reports on or before July 20, October 20, January 20, and April 20. Taxpayers should continue to file monthly reports until they receive the notice to begin filing quarterly reports.

Annually - When the average amount of tax for which the taxpayer is liable for the previous fiscal year (July 1 through June 30) does not exceed $25.00 per month, the Director may notify the taxpayer that the taxpayer may file one yearly report on or before January 20 for the preceding twelve (12) month period. Taxpayers should not change their method of filing until they receive the notice to begin filing annually.
Arkansas Law permits you to file on the next business day when the actual due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday
Arizona

Independent Local Nexus: No, counties and state-administered cities have dependent nexus.

State: Indian reservations within Arizona have independent nexus.
Independent Local Nexus: A remote seller has economic nexus if in the previous or current year, the gross sales generated from direct sales into Arizona is more than:

State: • $100,000 for 2021 and beyond.
A marketplace facilitator has economic nexus if it facilitates, for remote sellers or also on its own behalf, more than $100,000 in sales in the previous or current year.
Independent Local Nexus: Marketplace facilitators with economic or physical nexus are required to collect and remit transaction privilege tax on taxable sales in Arizona made through its platform on its own behalf and on behalf of marketplace sellers.
Economic Nexus Rules: No
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Yes: Some localities (Indian Reservations) impose Sales tax
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: Annually - <$2000 estimated annual combined Arizona, county and municipal transaction privilege tax (TPT) liability

State: Quarterly - $2000-$8,000 estimated annual combined Arizona, county and municipal tax liability
Monthly - >8000 estimated annual combined Arizona, county and municipal tax liability and Seasonal (eight months or less)
Generally must be filed no later than 20 days after the end of the period to which they relate; EFT initiated prior to the due date.
Arizona Law permits you to file on the next business day when the actual due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal Arizona holiday
California

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers are required to collect California tax if they have more than $500,000 in sales into California in the current or previous calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators are required to collect California tax if they make directly or facilitate more than $500,000 in sales into California in the current or previous calendar year.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: The CDTFA assigns businesses a filling frequency based on the anticipated sales tax liability at the time of the initial registration. The frequency can either be monthly, quarterly or annual. Businesses with an average taxable sales liability exceeding $17,000 per month, are required to submit monthly prepayments in addition to quarterly sales tax reports, such prepayments are due on the 24th of the corresponding month.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: The last day of the month following the filing period.

State: If the due date falls on a weekend or state holiday, the due date is the last business day in the month due.
Colorado

Economic Nexus Rules: Out-of-state retailers are considered to have a substantial nexus with Colorado for sales tax purposes if the retailer’s gross revenue from the sale of tangible personal property or services delivered into Colorado exceeds $100,000.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: In general, a marketplace facilitator must collect and remit all applicable state and state-administered local sales taxes for any sales made in Colorado through its marketplace. If a marketplace facilitator makes direct sales of its own goods and services through its marketplace, it bears the same sales tax obligations with respect to those sales as any other retailer.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually when sales tax liability is under $15 of tax per month. / Quarterly when sales tax liability is less between $15 and $600 per month. / Monthly when sales tax liability is greater than $600 per month.

State: Quarterly Returns:
1st quarter (January – March): due April 20
2nd quarter (April – June): due July 20
3rd quarter (July – September): due October 20
4th quarter (October – December): due January 20
Monthly Returns: due the 20th day of the month following the reporting month
Connecticut

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers making retail sales of tangible personal property or services have economic nexus, and are required to collect and remit sales tax, if during the preceding twelve-month period ending on September 30 they had gross receipts of at least $100,000 and made two hundred or more retail sales into Connecticut.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators are required to collect and remit sales tax on behalf of their marketplace sellers. Connecticut has defined a marketplace facilitator as any person who:

State: 1. Facilitated retail sales of at least $250,000 during the prior twelve-month period by marketplace sellers by providing a forum that lists or advertises tangible personal property subject to sale and use taxes or taxable services, including digital goods, for sale by such marketplace sellers;
2. Directly or indirectly through agreements or arrangements with third parties, collects receipt from the customer and remits payments to the marketplace sellers; and
3. Receives compensation or other consideration for its services.
Economic Nexus Rules: There is only one local taxing jurisdiction in Connecticut. The Mashantucket Pequot Tribal Nation imposes a 1.00% sales tax.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Connecticut does typically have a recurring sales tax holiday.

State: Effective for sales occurring the week starting with the third Sunday in August. Sales or purchases of clothing or footwear costing less than $100 per item are not subject to Connecticut sales and use taxes during the exclusion week. This one-week exclusion applies to clothing sales made by Connecticut and out-of-state retailers to Connecticut customers.
Independent Local Nexus: Quarterly when sales tax liability is less between $15 and $600 per month.
Economic Nexus Rules: Due on or before the last day of the month following the end of the filing period.

State: If the due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday or Legal Holiday, the return is due on the next business day.
Delaware
No Tax
Independent Local Nexus: Not applicable. Delaware has no state or local sales tax.
Economic Nexus Rules: Not applicable. Delaware has no state or local sales tax.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Not applicable. Delware has no state or local sales tax.
Sales Tax Holidays: No Sales Tax Holiday at the state level.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Monthly when sales tax liability is greater than $600 per month.

State: Florida

Independent Local Nexus: No, except for the 1% Merchant License Fee in the cities of Panama City and Panama City Beach.
Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Florida sales and use tax if their taxable sales from Florida sales in the current or previous calendar year is $100,000 or more.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect Florda sales tax and use tax if the facilitator made or facilitated $100,000 or more in Florida sales in the current or previous calendar year.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes, sales tax holidays are created through annual legislation. In 2024 there were the following sales tax holidays

State: Disaster Preparedness Sales Tax Holiday – 2 weeks in June and 2 weeks in August/September
Freedom Month– July 2024
Back to School Sales Tax Holiday – 2 weeks in August
Tool Time - first week of September
Independent Local Nexus: Annually when sales tax liability is $10 or less.

State: Semi Annually when the sales tax liability is $101-$500.
Quarterly when sales tax liability is $501-$1,000.
Monthly when sales tax liability is more than $1,000.
Independent Local Nexus: 20th, a return must be filed for each reporting period, even if no tax is due.

State: If the 20th falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or state or federal holiday, returns are timely if filed electronically, postmarked or hand-delivered on the first business day following the 20th.
Georgia

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Georgia sales tax when (1) they obtain gross revenue from sales into Georgia exceeding $100,000 in the previous or current calendar year or (2) they conduct 200 or more separate retail sales into Georgia in the previous or current calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect Georgia sales tax when (1) they obtain gross revenue from sales made or facilitated into Georgia exceeding $100,000 in the previous or current calendar year or (2) they conduct or facilitate 200 or more separate retail sales into Georgia in the previous or current calendar year.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually when sales tax liability is <= $50.00 per month

State: Quarterly when sales tax liability is <= $200 tax due per month
If the due date for filing a return falls on a weekend or holiday, the return is due on the following business day.

Initial Filing - Sales and use tax returns shall be filed on a calendar month basis during the first six (6) months from the date of registration. The initial return shall cover the month indicated on the registration application or the first month of the first transaction, whichever occurs earlier.

Special Period Filing - Dealers may be permitted to file the sales and use tax returns on a special period other than monthly after submitting a written request establishing reasonable grounds and having received written permission from the Commissioner or his designee. Dealers permitted to file on a special period basis must submit annually by November 1 to the Department the specific reporting periods for the next calendar year. Failure to submit the annual reporting periods to the Department timely may result in the loss of this privilege.
Iowa

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Iowa sales and applicable local option taxes if their gross revenue from Iowa sales in the current or previous calendar year is $100,000 or more.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect Iowa sales tax and applicable local option taxes if the facilitator made or facilitated $100,000 or more in Iowa sales in the current or previous calendar year.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes, on Select Clothing and Footwear. The sales tax holiday is held on the first Friday and Saturday in August.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually when sales tax liability is < $1200 per year

State: Monthly when sales tax liability is $1200 or greater per year
Independent Local Nexus: Annual: Return and payment due no later than January 31 following each calendar year

State: Monthly: Due on or before the last day of the following month.
If a due date falls on a weekend, federal holiday, or legal holiday as defined in Iowa code section 4.1(34), the due date will extend to the next working day.
Idaho

Economic Nexus Rules: Retailers without a physical presence must collect Idaho sales tax when their sales in Idaho exceed $100,000 in the current or previous year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators without a physical presence must collect Idaho sales tax when the combined total of their own sales in Idaho and their third-party sales in Idaho exceed $100,000 in the current or previous year.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: The Idaho state Tax Commission assigns a filing frequency at the time of registration for new businesses.  The filing frequency is determined based on the anticpated liability of the seller and can either be monthly, quarterly, semi-annual or annual.  Businesses with larger sales amounts into Idaho are required to file more frequently.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: The filing date for all sales or use tax returns is the twentieth day of the calendar month immediately following the last day of the reporting period.

State: If the twentieth is a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday, the return is due on the next following day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or legal holiday.
Illinois

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers with no physical presence whose cumulative gross receipts from sales of tangible personal property to purchasers in Illinois are $100,000 or more must register with the Illinois Department of Revenue to begin collecting and remitting Illinois Sales and Use Tax for sales made to Illinois purchasers (effective 1/1/2026).
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect Retailer's Occupation Tax when they facilitate gross sales of $100,000 to purchasers in Illinois during the previous 12 months (effective 1/1/2026).
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually when sales tax liability is <= $50 tax due per month.

State: Quarterly when sales tax liability is $50 to $200 tax due per month.
Monthly when sales tax liability is $200 or greater tax due per month.
If a due date falls on Saturday, Sunday, or a holiday, the due date shall be the next business date.
Indiana

Economic Nexus Rules: A retail merchant wihtout physical presence in Indiana must collect and remit Indiana sales tax where its has gross revenue exceeding $100,000 in the preceding or current calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: A marketplace facilitator lacking physical presence in Indiana must register and collect Indiana sales tax on behalf of sellers where its has gross revenue exceeding $100,000 in the preceding or current calendar year.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: The Indiana Department of Revenue assigns a filing frequency at the time of registration for new businesses.  The filing frequency is determined based on the seller's average monthly sales tax liability throughout the year and can be adjusted by the Department at anytime. The available frequencies are monthly and annual.  Businesses with larger sales amounts into Indiana are required to file more frequently.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: Annual - January 31st. Monthly - 20th of the month for early filers, 30th of the month for monthly filers. If the due date falls on a weekend, federal or state holiday, the payment is due on the next business day.

State: Kansas

Economic Nexus Rules: On May 3, 2021, the Kansas legislature overrode the Governor's veto on Senate Bill 50. As such, effective July 1, 2021, out of state retailers are considered to be "doing business" in the state if they have more than $100,000 of cumulative gross receipts fom sales to customers in Kansas in the preceding or current calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: A marketplace facilitator lacking physical presence in Kansas must register and collect tax on behalf of sellers if it meets the conditions for economic nexus, detailed above.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually when tax liability is $0 - $1,000

State: Quarterly when tax liability is $1,000.01 - $5,000
Monthly when tax liability is $5,000.01 or more
If any due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday, substitute the next regular work day. If the tax payment is due on bank holiday or a weekend, the payment is due the next business day.
Kentucky

Economic Nexus Rules: Kentucky requires remote retailers to collect and remit sales tax if the remote retailer made 200 or more sales into the state or had more than $100,000 in gross receipts from sales into the state during the previous or current calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace providers with $100,000 in gross receipts or 200 or more separate transactions sourced to Kentucky in the previous or current calendar year are required to register for a Kentucky sales and use tax account and collect Kentucky tax on all sales through their marketplaces. A marketplace provider should include both its own sales and the sales it facilitates when calculating these thresholds
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: No localities impose sales and use tax. However, some localities impose motor vehicle rental tax or meals tax.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually when sales tax liability is $0 to $125.00 in average monthly tax liability.

State: Monthly when sales tax liability is $125.01 to $1,119.99 in average monthly tax liability.
Monthly when sales tax liability is $1,200 to $9,999.99 in average monthly tax liability.
Monthly (with prepayment) when sales tax liability is more than $10,000 in average monthly tax liability - Accelerated Sales and Use Tax Return.
Independent Local Nexus: 20th of the month following the filing period.

State: If the due date falls on a weekend or legal holiday, the return is due the next business day.
Louisiana

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers who have more than $100,000 in sales or more than 200 separate sales into the Louisiana are required to collect state and local sales taxes. Prior to July 1, 2020 remote sellers collected sales tax at a special combined state and local rate 8.45%.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators are required to collect Louisiana sales tax on behalf of sellers when the marketplace makes or facilitates more than $100,000 in sales in Louisiana.
Sales Tax Holidays: Louisiana has a Second Amendment Sales Tax Holiday, exempting from sales tax sales of firearms, ammunition, and hunting supplies, that occurs each calendar year on the first consecutive Friday through Sunday of September.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Quaterly - Tax liability < $500 per month

State: Monthly - Tax laibility => $500 per month
If a due dates falls on a weekend or holiday, the return is due on the next business day and becomes deliquent the next day thereafter.
Massachusetts

Economic Nexus Rules: A tax collection and remittance obligation is created if a seller has $100,000 or more in sales in Massachusetts.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Massachusetts marketplace liability rules require “marketplace facilitators” and “remote marketplace facilitators” with sales exceeding $100,000 to report, collect and remit on all such sales, whether such sales are direct sales by the marketplace facilitator or sales facilitated for a marketplace seller.
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: Localities do not impose sales tax. However, many impose meals tax.
Sales Tax Holidays: Massachusetts has an annual “sales tax holiday weekend,” i.e., two consecutive days during which most purchases made by individuals for personal use will not be subject to Massachusetts sales or use taxes. The sales tax holiday occurs annually, on a two-day weekend designated by the general court by joint resolution no later than June 15th. If the general court fails to adopt such a joint resolution, the Commissioner of Revenue shall, no later than July 1st, designate a two-day weekend in August of that year as the annual sales tax holiday.

State: For the purposes of the annual sales tax holiday, tangible personal property shall not include telecommunications services, tobacco products subject to the excise imposed by M.G.L. c. 64C, marijuana or marijuana products, as defined in M.G.L. c. 94G, § 1, alcoholic beverages, as defined in M.G.L. c. 138, § 1, gas, steam, electricity, motor vehicles, motorboats, meals or a single item the price of which is in excess of $2,500.
Independent Local Nexus: Annually when sales tax liability is $100 or less

State: Quarterly when sales tax liability is between $101 and $1,200
Monthly when sales tax liability is greater than $1,200 excluding meals
Businesses incurring use tax liabilities who are not registered vendors may file a Business Use Tax Return (Form ST-10) which is due annually by April 15th.
If a due date falls on a weekend or holiday the due date will be moved to the next business day
Maryland

Economic Nexus Rules: Persons lacking physical presence in the state are deemed to be engaging in the business of an out-of-state vendor required to collect sales tax if, in the previous calendar year, gross revenue from taxable sales made into the state in the prior calendar year exceed $100,000 or more than 200 separate transactions for taxable sales were made.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Remote marketplace facilitators are required to collect sales and use tax on behalf of marketplace sellers if, in the previous calendar year, gross revenue from taxable sales made into the state in the prior calendar year exceed $100,000 or more than 200 separate transactions for taxable sales were made. Collections made on behalf of marketplace sellers shall be reported separately from collections on sales made directly by the marketplace facilitator or an affiliate. Marketplace sellers are not required to collect applicable taxes on sales when marketplace facilitators collect and remit on their behalf.
Sales Tax Holidays: The second Sunday of August to the following Saturday is designated as Shop Maryland Tax-Free Week each year. That means qualifying apparel and footwear $100 or less, per item, are exempt from the state sales tax. The first $40 of a backpack or book bag purchase is also tax free. / Additionally, there is a tax-free three-day weekend every February during which the state sales tax will not apply to the sale of any Energy Star Product listed here, or solar water heater.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Filing thresholds are at the discretion of the Comptroller upon end-of-fiscal-year review. New accounts are typically quarterly.

State: If a due dates falls on a Saturday or Sunday or holiday, the report is due on the next business day.
Maine

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers are required to register and collect sales tax if, during the current or previous calendar year, their gross revenues from Maine sales of tangible personal property, products transferred electronically, or taxable services exceeded $100,000.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: A marketplace facilitator is considered a retailer for each sale of tangible personal property or taxable service for delivery into Maine that the marketplace facilitator facilitates on or through its marketplace, including those they facilitate for sellers not required to register for Maine sales and use tax.

State: A marketplace facilitator without substantial physical presence in Maine is required to register to collect and remit Maine sales and use tax if:
The marketplace facilitator's gross sales from delivery of tangible personal property or taxable services into Maine in the previous calendar year or current calendar year exceeds $100,000.
Economic Nexus Rules: No
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Additionally, there is a tax-free three-day weekend every February during which the state sales tax will not apply to the sale of any Energy Star Product listed here, or solar water heater.

State: Annually - Less than $50.00 average monthly liability
Semi-Annually - Less than $100.00 average monthly liability
Quarterly - Between $100.00 and $599.99 average monthly liability
Monthly - $600.00 or greater average monthly liability
SPT (Service Provider Tax Return)
Annually - Less than $50.00 average monthly liability
Semi-Annually - Less than $100.00 average monthly liability
Quarterly - Between $100.00 and $599.99 average monthly liability
Monthly - $600.00 or greater average monthly liability
Independent Local Nexus: 15th of the month following the end of the tax period

State: If the 15th falls on a weekend or legal holiday in Maine, then the due date is changed to the next business day.
Michigan

Economic Nexus Rules: A remote seller has nexus with Michigan and is required to report and pay sales or use tax if, in the previous calendar year, the remote seller made over $100,000 of sales to, or more than 200 transactions with, Michigan customers.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: A marketplace facilitator or marketplace seller will have economic presence in Michigan and be required to report and pay sales or use tax if, in the previous calendar year, it has over $100,000 in sales to, or more than 200 transactions with, Michigan customers.
Sales Tax Holidays: No Sales Tax Holiday at the state level.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: No

State: Quarterly: Due on or before the 20th day of the following month
Part-quarterly: Due on or before the 20th day of the month following the quarter
Monthly: Due the 20th day of the calendar month for the preceding calendar month
If the due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or a legal holiday for Michigan, the payment is due the next business day.
Minnesota

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers who have made 200 or more retail sales or $100,000 or more in retail sales in Minnesota during the prior 12-month period must collect Minnesota sales tax.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators who have made or facilitated 200 or more retail sales or $100,000 or more in retail sales in Minnesota during the prior 12-month period must collect Minnesota sales tax.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Monthly - More than $500 per month

State: Quarterly - $100 to $500 per month
Annually - Less than $100 per month
Independent Local Nexus: Monthly - 20th day of the following month. / Quarterly (2025) - April 21, July 21, October 20, January 20, 2026 (2025) / Annually - February 5 of the following calendar year. / Weekend and Holiday rules - Returns and payments may be filed or postmarked (as applicable) on the next business day to be considered timely.

State: Missouri

Economic Nexus Rules: Effective January 1, 2023, vendors with cumulative gross receipts of at least $100,000 from taxable sales of tangible personal property in the previous 12-month period must collect the state vendor use tax rate and any applicable local use taxes.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Effective January 1, 2023, marketplace facilitators must register to collect and remit use tax if cumulative gross receipts exceed $100,000 from taxable sales of tangible personal property during the previous 12-month period.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes, two annual holidays.

State: Show Me Green Sales Tax Holiday (April 19th through April 25th) - sales of qualifying Energy Star certified new appliances are exempt from state sales tax.

Back-to-School Sales Tax Holiday (first Friday in August and continues through the following Sunday) - Certain back-to-school purchases, such as clothing, school supplies, computers, and other items as defined by the statute, are exempt from sales tax.
Independent Local Nexus: Annually when quarterly state sales tax liability is less than $200.00.

State: Quarterly when quarterly state sales tax liability is $200.00 or more but less than $500.00 per month.
Monthly when monthly state sales tax liability is $500.00 or more.
Independent Local Nexus: Annually - February 5 of the following calendar year

State: Mississippi
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: Weekend and Holiday rules - Returns and payments may be filed or postmarked (as applicable) on the next business day to be considered timely.

Economic Nexus Rules: Businesses located out of the state that have sales into the state of Mississippi that exceed $250,000 over any twelve month period are considered to have substantial economic presence in the state and are required to register with the Mississippi Department of Revenue in order to collect and remit tax.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators that exceed $250,000 in sales into the state are required to collect and remit tax.
Sales Tax Holidays: Mississippi has two annual sales tax holidays.

State: The first takes place from the second Friday in July through the following Sunday and applies to the sale of articles of clothing or footwear if the sales price of a single item is less than $100.

The second - the Mississippi Second Amendment Weekend - takes place from the last Friday in August through the following Sunday and applies to sales of firearms, ammunition, and certain hunting supplies.
Independent Local Nexus: The filing frequencies of Sales and Use Tax Returns are based on the annual amount of taxes annually remitted by the taxpayer to the DOR. If the annual total payment is less than $600.00, annual returns are filed. If the annual total payment is between $600.00 and $3,599.00, quarterly returns are filed. If the annual remittance is more than $3,599.00, monthly returns are filed.​​​​
Economic Nexus Rules: 20th

State: If a due date falls on a weekend or holiday, the due date becomes the next business day.
Montana
No Tax
Independent Local Nexus: Not applicable. Montana has no state or local sales tax.
Economic Nexus Rules: Not applicable. Montana has no state or local sales tax.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Not applicable. Montana has no state or local sales tax.
Sales Tax Holidays: No Sales Tax Holiday at the state level.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: No

State: North Carolina

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect North Carolina sales tax if their sales into the state exceed $100,000 in the current or previous year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect North Carolina sales tax if their sales into the state exceed $100,000 in the current or previous year. Marketplace facilitators are considered the retailer of each marketplace-facilitated sale it makes and is liable for collecting and remitting the sales and use tax on all such sales.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Quarterly when sales tax liability is <= $100

State: Monthly when sales tax liability is <= $$20,000 per month and at least $100 per month
Monthly Filing with Prepayment - at least $20,000 a month in state and local sales and use taxes
Independent Local Nexus: Quarterly - The returns with remittance of tax are due on or before the last day of January, April, July, and October for the preceding three-month period.

State: Monthly - on or before the 20th day of each month for all taxes due for the preceding calendar month. The taxes due must be remitted with the monthly return.
Monthly Filing with Prepayment - on or before the 20th day of each month.

North Carolina will consider any document with a due date on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday as being timely filed if delivered in person or mailed or electronically submitted to the Department on or before the next business day after the Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday.
North Dakota

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect North Dakota sales tax if their sales into the state exceed $100,000 in the current or previous calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect North Dakota sales tax if their facilitated and direct sales exceed $100,000 in the current or previous calendar year.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Assigned by the Office of the Tax Commissioner: may be monthly, quarterly, or annually
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: Returns are due the last day of the month following the tax reporting period.

State: If the last day of the month falls on a weekend or legal holiday, the due date is the next business day.
Nebraska

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers selling tangible property and services subject to Nebraska sales tax must obtain a sales tax permit from the Nebraska Department of Revenue and begin collecting and remitting Nebraska state and local sales taxes if their retail sales, including sales made through a Multivendor Marketplace Platform (MMP), in Nebraska exceeded $100,000 in the previous year or current calendar year, or if the seller made 200 or more separate Nebraska retail sales transactions in the prior calendar year or current calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Multivendor Marketplace Platforms that exceed the threshold(s) mentioned above are retailers and must obtain a permit and begin collecting and remitting Nebraska and local sales taxes on sales they facilitate for others that are delivered or sourced to a Nebraska address.

State: Nebraska has local jurisdictions that impose additional sales/use taxes, as well as Good Life Districts, which change the state sales tax rate to 2.75%
Economic Nexus Rules: NE-10

State: Annually when yearly sales tax liability is less than $900.00.
Quarterly when yearly sales tax liability is greater than or equal to $900.00 and less than $3,000.00
Monthly when yearly sales tax liability is greater than $3,000.00
NE-2
Liability greater than $0.00 - Follow NE-10 filing frequency
NE-6
Liability greater than $0.00 - Follow NE-10 filing frequency
Independent Local Nexus: 20th of the month following the end of the tax period

State: If the due date falls on Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday, the return or voucher may be filed on the first business day after the due date.
New Hampshire
No Tax
Independent Local Nexus: Not applicable. New Hampshire has no state or local sales tax.
Economic Nexus Rules: Not applicable. New Hampshire has no state or local sales tax.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Not applicable. New Hampshire has no state or local sales tax.
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: New Hampshire has no local sales tax.
Sales Tax Holidays: Not applicable. New Hampshire has no state or local sales tax.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Not applicable. New Hampshire has no state or local sales tax.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: Not applicable. New Hampshire has no state or local sales tax.

State: New Jersey

Economic Nexus Rules: For sales made on and after November 1, 2018, a remote seller that makes a retail sale of tangible personal property, specified digital products, or services delivered into New Jersey must register, collect, and remit New Jersey Sales Tax if the remote seller meets either of the following criteria (the economic threshold):

State: -The remote seller's gross revenue from sales of tangible personal property, specified digital products, or services delivered into New Jersey during the current or prior calendar year, exceeds $100,000; or
-The remote seller sold tangible personal property, specified digital products, or services delivered into New Jersey in 200 or more separate transactions during the current or prior calendar year.
A remote seller that does not meet either of these criteria does not have to register with the Division of Revenue and Enterprise Services to collect and remit New Jersey Sales Tax.
Independent Local Nexus: Remote sellers are not required to collect and remit Sales Tax on the sale of tangible personal property, specified digital products, or services delivered into New Jersey when sold through a marketplace, because the new law requires the marketplace facilitator to collect and remit Sales Tax on all marketplace transactions. The marketplace is required to collect and remit tax, regardless of whether the marketplace seller is above or below either of the economic thresholds.
Economic Nexus Rules: Yes - Through SST Progrm Participation
Marketplace Facilitator Information: No local sales tax however some localities impose tax on motor vehicle rentals or meals. Additionally there is a tax imposed for Kapkowski Road Landfill Reclamation District Project.
CSP Program: Yes - Back to School - 10 day period starting on the 9th day preceeding 1st Monday in September
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: ST-18B (Business Use Return) - Annually All businesses that owe use tax and do not owe sales tax must file

State: ST-50 (SUT Return) - Quarterly when sales tax liability is $30,000 or less prior year sales tax liability
ST-51 (SUT Return) - Monthly when sales tax liability is greater than $30,000 prior year sales tax liability
May 1/Annual
If a due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday, the filing is due on the next business day.
New Mexico

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers are required to collect gross receipts tax when their total gross receipts from sales sourced to the state are $100,000 or more in the previous calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace providers with sales into New Mexico over $100,000 must collect tax on behalf of their marketplace sellers. New Mexico permits marketplace sellers to deduct receipts for sales that are facilitated through a marketplace provider, so long as the seller obtains documentation from the provider indicating that the provider is registered with the department as has remitted or will remit taxes due on gross receipts from applicable transactions.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes - Back to School - end of July
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Semiannually - When combined taxes are less that $1200 for the semiannual period or an average of less that $200 per month for the 6-month period

State: Quarterly - When combined taxes for the quarter are less that $600 or an average of less than $200 per month in the quarter
Monthly - When combined taxes average more than $200 per month
Navajo Nation - Quarterly
If your due date falls on a weekend, a legal, state or national holiday, the payment is due on the next business day.
Navajo Nation - 15th
If a due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or a legal holiday, then the due date becomes the next working day.
Nevada

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers are required to collect Nevada sales and use tax if in the previous or current calendar year they have made more than $100,000 of retail sales in Nevada, or they have made 200 or more separate retail transactions in to the state.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: A marketplace facilitators are required to collect Nevada sales tax on all its facilitated and remote sales when its total of all such sales exceeds $100,000 or 200 separate transaction in the preceding or current calendar year.)
Sales Tax Holidays: No Sales Tax Holiday at the state level.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Sales/Purchases => $10,000.00 - Monthly

State: Sales/Purchases < $10,000.00 - Quarterly (Quarterly filers may choose to file monthly if they so wish)
Sales/Purchases < $1,500.00 in the immediate preceding year - Annually
Purchases => $10,000.00 - Monthly
Purchases < $10,000.00 - Quarterly
Purchases < $1,000.00 - Annually
Independent Local Nexus: For Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually filling - Payment is due the last day of the month following the report period

State: Weekend Rules - No rules apparently available. Best practice is likely to be to postmark/submit taxes prior to an end of month holiday/weekend date.
New York

Economic Nexus Rules: A business that has no physical presence in New York State is required to register as a sales tax vendor and collect and timely remit the applicable state and local sales tax if in the immediately preceding four sales tax quarters it has made more than $500,000 in sales of tangible personal property delivered in the state, and conducted more than 100 sales of tangible personal property delivered in the state.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: A marketplace provider is required to collect and remit sales tax on all taxable sales of tangible personal property that it facilitates for marketplace sellers, regardless of whether the marketplace seller is required to be registered. Marketplace providers are not liable for the sales tax on services provided by marketplace sellers. Marketplace providers without physical presence are required to register for sales tax if the make or facilitate $500,000 in sales of tangible personal property delivered in the state, and make or facilitate more than 100 sales of tangible personal property delivered in the state.
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: Localities (cities, counties and school districts) are authorized to impose specified taxes, including sales and use taxes, within rate limits. N.Y. Tax Law §1107; N.Y. Tax Law § 1210.

State: An additional tax (0.375%) is imposed in the Metropolitan Commuter Transportation District, which includes New York City and the Counties of Dutchess, Nassau, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Suffolk and Westchester. N.Y. Tax Law §1109; N.Y. Tax Law §1210.
There are 57 counties (excluding those wholly within NYC) and approximately 18 cities which impose local sales taxes.
Annually when annual tax due is less than or equal to $3,000.
Quarterly when annual tax due is greater than $3,000.
Monthly(Part Quarterly) when quarterly taxable sales and purchases are greater than or equal to $300,000 per quarter.
PrompTax (ST-810)
Quarterly when annual sales and use tax liabilities are greater than $500,000. File quarterly, electronic payment due monthly.
20th of March
20th of month following taxable period
20th of month following taxable period
PrompTax (ST-810)

The tax due date is always three business days following the period end date for which you are reporting, Period end date is always day 22 of the current month. ACH credit transaction must be initiated no later than two business days following period end date for which you are reporting.
Ohio

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Ohio sales tax when (1) they have gross sales into Ohio exceeding $100,000 or (2) they have 200 or more separate transactions into Ohio.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect Ohio sales when (1) they make or facilitate gross sales into Ohio exceeding $100,000 or (2) they make or facilitate 200 or more separate transactions into Ohio.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes. Expanded STH occuring for at least three days in July/August.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annual liability exceeds $75,000 a month - Monthly

State: Less than $15,000 in quarterly liability - Quarterly
Less than $1,200 per six-month period - Semi-Annually
Independent Local Nexus: 23rd of the month following the reporting period

State: Sales tax payment is due the 23rd of the following month unless the 23rd falls on a weekend or a holiday. In that case, it would be due on the following business day.
Oklahoma

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Oklahoma sales tax when make $100,000 or more in sales into Oklahoma in the current or preceding calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators who make or facilitate $10,000 or more in sales into Oklahoma in the current or preceding calendar year must either (1) elect to report and remit Oklahoma sales tax or (2) comply with notice and reporting requirements.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes. Annual "Back to School" Sales Tax Holiday occurring during the first weekend in August.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Monthly (most businesses) when sales tax liability whose returns average $2500Monthly (most businesses) when sales tax liability whose returns average $2500. / Semi Annually when sales tax liability is $0 to $50.00 (month
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: The 20th of the month following the filing period.

State: If a due date falls on Saturday, Sunday, a holiday, or dates when the Federal Reserve Banks are closed, such due date shall be considered to be the next business date.
Oregon
No Tax
Economic Nexus Rules: No economic nexus legislation has been enacted.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: No marketplace facilitator legislation has been enacted.
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: Some localities impose Meals or Motor Vehicle Rental Taxes.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Not applicable. Oregon has no state or local sales tax.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: Not applicable. Oregon has no state or local sales tax.

State: Pennsylvani

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers have economic presence and must collect and remit sales tax if they made more than $100,000 in annual Pennsylvania gross sales. Remote sellers should determine if they are required to collect sales tax on a year-to-year basis. For the year 2020 and beyond, the collection period is April 1 through March 31, using the previous calendar year to determine economic presence.

State: Marketplace facilitators must include both their own sales and sales facilitated on behalf of marketplace sellers when determining if they have economic presence.
Independent Local Nexus: A marketplace facilitator with physical or economic presence in Pennsylvania must collect and remit sales tax on all taxable sales facilitated through its marketplace.
Economic Nexus Rules: No
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Yes - (Allegheny County and the City of Philadelphia)
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: Each year the Department of Revenue reviews the filing status of all sales tax licensees and makes adjustments based on your remittance from the third quarter of the previous year.

State: Monthly returns – Licensees whose actual tax liability is less than $25,000, but greater than $600 per quarter, must file monthly.
Quarterly returns – Licensees whose total tax liability is less than $600 in the third calendar quarter, but greater than $300 annually, must file quarterly.
Semi-annual returns – Licensees whose total tax liability is $300 or less annually must file semi- annually.

If a payment due date falls on a bank holiday, the day after the bank holiday or weekend, the tax payment must be initiated the business day prior to the bank holiday or weekend so that funds are available immediately on the first business day after the bank holiday or weekend.
Rhode Island

Economic Nexus Rules: All remote sellers are required to collect and remit Sales and Use tax to the State of Rhode Island if within the current or previous year the sale of tangible personal property, taxable services, or specified digital products for delivery into Rhode Island exceeded one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) or two hundred (200) transactions.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators shall collect sales and use tax on all sales made through the marketplace to purchasers in Rhode Island whether or not the marketplace seller (1) has or is required to have a permit to make sales at retail or (2) would have been required to collect and remit sales and use tax had the sale not been made through the marketplace provider (ie: if within the current or previous year the sale of tangible personal property, taxable services, or specified digital products for delivery into Rhode Island exceeded one hundred thousand dollars ($100,000) or two hundred (200) transactions.)
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Quarterly when total tax liability <= $200.00/month averaged over the preceding six months

State: Monthly - All who are not authorized to file quarterly
Independent Local Nexus: Quarterly - Last day of the quarter for the preceding three months.

State: Monthly - 20th of the month following the filing period
If due date falls on a weekend or holiday, due next non-holiday business day.
South Carolina

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers whose gross revenue exceeds $100,000 in the previous or current calendar year are required to collect South Carolina sales tax.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace providers must collect South Carolina sales tax if their gross revenue exceeds $100,000 in the current or previous calendar year.
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: Yes - County level and City of Myrtle Beach / City and Country level Hospitality (Meals) Tax
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes, an annual Back to School sales tax holiday takes place during the first weekend of August.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Sales and Use Tax accounts are set up with a monthly filing frequency. Quarterly and annual filing frequencies must be approved by the SCDOR. The request must be submitted in writing.

State: South Dakota
Local Taxing Jurisdictions: City and County level Hospitality (Meals) Tax

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect South Dakota sales tax if their gross revenue from sales into South Dakota exceeds $100,000 in the current or previous calendar year.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect South Dakota sales tax if their gross revenue from sales into South Dakota exceeds $100,000 in the current or previous calendar year.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Any business with a physical presence in South Dakota is required to collect sales tax and file a return. There is no threshold.

State: Businesses who obtain a license will be notified of their filing frequency by the South Dakota of Revenue.
Independent Local Nexus: 20th day of the month in which the return is due

State: If the 20th falls on a weekend or holiday, the return is due on next non-holiday business day
Tennessee

Economic Nexus Rules: Out-of-state dealers with no physical presence in Tennessee who make sales exceeding $100,000 to consumers in Tennessee during the previous 12-month period must register with the Tennessee Department of Revenue and begin collecting and remitting Tennessee sales and use tax. For purposes of applying the $100,000 threshold, dealers should include all retail sales, including exempt retail sales, but should exclude sales for resale.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Beginning October 1, 2020, marketplace facilitators must collect tax when they facilitate total sales of more than $100,000 to consumers in Tennessee during the previous 12 months. Additionally, marketplace facilitators must source sales of tangible personal property on their marketplace by destination sourcing.
CSP Program: Yes, please note, Tennessee is the only associate member of the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Project.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes, an annual Back-to-School sales tax holiday (clothing, school supplies, etc.). In previous years, there have also been sales tax holidays on Food Items as well as Gun Safety Equipment.

State: TN publishes information regarding each of their sales tax holidays here:
https://www.tn.gov/revenue/taxes/sales-and-use-tax/sales-tax-holiday.html

Sales Tax accounts are automatically set up for monthly filing. However, effective 7/1/21, businesses with a sales tax liability over a 12-month period that averages $1,000 or less per month may choose to file monthly or quarterly.
20th

The 20th day of the month following the filing period (i.e. May for April filings). Annual returns are due on January 20th. Tennessee taxpayers to file on the next business day when the actual due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday.
Modified 7/30/24

For Monthly returns, the 20th day of the month following the filing period (i.e. May 20 for April filings). For Quarterly returns, the 20th day of the month following the end of the quarter (January 20, April 20, July 20, October 20). Annual returns are due on January 20th. Tennessee taxpayers may file on the next business day when the actual due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday.
Texas

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Texas sales tax if their sales exceed $500,000 in the preceding twelve calendar months.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Texas law requires marketplace providers to collect and remit state and local sales and use tax on all third-party sales.
Sales Tax Holidays: Texas has three annual sales tax holiday:

State: a) The first holiday takes place from the Saturday before the last Monday in April and ends on the last Monday in April. The holiday applies to the sale of emergency preparedness supplies.
* https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/publications/98-1017.php

b) The second holiday(s) takes place from the Saturday preceding the last Monday in May (Memorial Day) and ends on the last Monday in May and applies to sales of certain Energy Star, water-conserving and, WaterSense products.
* https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/publications/96-1331.php
* https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/publications/98-1018.php
c) The third holiday takes place the first weekend in August and applies to clothing, footwear and, school supplies sold for less than $100.
* https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/publications/98-490/
Independent Local Nexus: Annual - Taxpayers who collect < $1000 in state sales tax per year (requires authorization from Comptroller's office)

State: Quarterly - Taxpayers who collect < $1500 in a calendar quarter
Monthly - Taxpayers who collect >= $500 in state sales tax in a single month
Independent Local Nexus: 20th of the month following the filing period

State: If a due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday or legal holiday, the next working day is the due date.
Utah

Independent Local Nexus: No, counties and cities have dependent nexus.

State: The Navajo Nation Indian Reservation has independent nexus
Independent Local Nexus: Utah requires a remote seller to collect and pay Utah sales tax if, in either the previous or the current calendar year, the remote seller receives gross revenue of more than $100,000 from the sale of tangible personal property, products transferred electronically, or services for storage, use, or consumption in Utah.
Economic Nexus Rules: A marketplace facilitator that has Utah nexus for their facilitated sales must have a Utah sales tax license and collect, report and pay sales tax on facilitated sales.

State: A marketplace facilitator can have nexus on its own direct sales, on sales it facilitates, or both. Therefore, facilitators must determine nexus separately for their direct sales and their facilitated sales.
Economic Nexus Rules: Yes
Marketplace Facilitator Information: No
CSP Program: For the TC-62M and TC-62S, Sales and Use Tax Returns:

State: $96,001 or more - Monthly Filer with mandatory EFT Payments
$1,001 to $50,000 - Quarterly
$1,000 or less - Annual
Independent Local Nexus: For monthly filers with mandatory EFT payments - returns are due the last day of month following end of period.

State: For monthly filers - returns are due the last day of month following end of period.
For quarterly filers - returns are due on April 30, July 31, Oct. 31, and Jan. 31.
For annual filers - returns are due on January 31st
When a due date falls on a weekend or legal holiday, the return is due the next business day
Virginia

Economic Nexus Rules: A remote seller is required to collect sales and use tax if their annual direct sales to Virginia consumers total more than $100,000 or 200 or more separate transactions.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Receipts collected by a marketplace provider are gross receipts, and are subject to tax if the marketplace provider has annual sales of products and services into the state of more than $100,000 or has more than 200 separate transactions delivered in West Virginia.
Sales Tax Holidays: Combined Sales Tax Holiday begins on the first Friday in August each year and ends at on the following Sunday . Covers: Hurricane preparedness items, clothing/school supplies, and Energy Star & WaterSense items
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: The Virginia Department of Taxation will notify businesses about their corresponding filling frequency which will be on a monthly or quarterly basis, depending on the businesses anticipated taxable sales at the moment of registration. Filling frequency can change based on the occurring tax liability, in such case the VA Department of Taxation will send the corresponding notification.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: 20th of the month following the filing period.

State: If the due date falls on a weekend or state holiday, the due date is the next business day.
Vermont

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect and remit Vermont sales tax when they have made sales into Vermont of at least $100,000 or totaling at least 200 individual sales transactions during any preceding 12-month period.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect and remit Vermont sales tax when they have made or facilitated sales into Vermont of at least $100,000 or totaling at least 200 individual sales transactions during any preceding 12-month period. Marketplace facilitators must inform sellers on its marketplaces that they will collect and remit tax on their behalf.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually - No threshold information. Must be assigned by the Department of Tax.

State: Quarterly - Less than $500 previous year tax liability
Monthly - Greater than $500 previous year tax liability.
Independent Local Nexus: 25th of the month following the filing period. Annual returns are due on January 25.

State: If the due date falls on a weekend or holiday, the due date will be the following business day.
Washington

Economic Nexus Rules: Businesses without physical presence in the state are required to register for tax if their cumulative gross receipts attributed to Washington exceed $100,000. Cumulative gross receipts include all of a person's gross income of the business attributed to Washington and is not limited to the business's retail sales.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators making or facilitating sales to Washington consumers must register for sales tax if they have more than $100,000 in combined gross receipts sourced or attributed to Washington, have marketplace sellers with physical presence in Washington, are organized or commercially domiciled in Washington, or have physical presence in Washington. Marketplace facilitators must collect retail sales tax, and any other taxes and fees imposed on the buyer, on behalf of the marketplace seller.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: State assigned. Filing frequencies are assigned based on what you estimate for your yearly businedd income

State: Quarterly - The end of the month following the tax quarter (e.g., the quarter 1 (Jan, Feb, Mar) tax return is due April 30
Annual - April 15, please note; due to Covid 19 the due date has been extended to June 15 for the reporting on 2019 transactions.
If the due date falls on a weekend or legal holiday, the due date is extended to the next business day.
Wisconsin

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Wisconsin sales tax if their annual sales into the state are more than $100,000.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: A marketplace facilitator is required to collect Wisconsin sales tax if their annual sales they made on behalf of a third party exceed $100,000.
Sales Tax Holidays: No Sales Tax Holiday at the state level.
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Filing frequency is determined based on remittances for a 12-month period ending October 31, 2021, as follows:

State: Early monthly - $3,601 or more/quarter
Monthly - $1,201 - $3,600/quarter
Quarterly - $601 - $1,200/quarter
Annual - $600/year or less
Independent Local Nexus: For Monthly, Quarterly, and Annual filers - Sales and use tax returns are due the last day of the month following the reporting period

State: For Early monthly filers - Sales and use tax returns are due by the 20th of Month following the reporting period.
When the regular due date falls on a weekend or legal holiday, the due date is extended to the business day immediately following the weekend or legal holiday.
West Virginia

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect West Virginia sales tax when the remote seller has annual sales of products and services into the state of more than $100,000 or has more than 200 separate transactions delivered in West Virginia.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Receipts collected by a marketplace provider are gross receipts, and are subject to tax if the marketplace provider has annual sales of products and services into the state of more than $100,000 or has more than 200 separate transactions delivered in West Virginia.
Sales Tax Holidays: Yes, an annual Back-to-School sales tax holiday takes place the first weekend of August. More info can be found here: https://tax.wv.gov/Business/Pages/SalesTaxHoliday.aspx
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Filling frequencies will depend on the sales tax liability. Newly registered businesses will be automatically assigned a monthly filling frequency on their first year. After the first year the filling frequency can change to yearly if sales tax liability is $600 or less per year, or to quarterly if sales tax liability is $3,000 or less per year.
Filing Due Dates / Weekend Rules: 20th of the month following the filing period.

State: If the due date falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or holiday, the taxpayer shall initiate the transaction the preceding business day
Wyoming

Economic Nexus Rules: Remote sellers must collect Wyoming sales tax when their gross revenue from sales into Wyoming exceeds $100,000.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators must collect Wyoming sales tax when their gross revenue from sales made or facilitated into Wyoming exceeds $100,000.
Sales Tax Holidays: No
Filing Thresholds / Frequencies: Annually - Between $0.00 and $50.00 in collected tax per month.

State: Quarterly - Between $50.00 and $150.00 in collected tax per month.
Monthly - More than $150.00 in collected tax per month.
Independent Local Nexus: All returns must be postmarked by the last day of the month following the last month in the reporting period.

State: If the last day of the month falls on a weekend or holiday the returns must be postmarked by the following business day.
District of Columbia

Economic Nexus Rules: The District of Columbia requires remote sellers to collect and remit sales tax if in the previous or current calendar year they had gross receipts from all retail sales delivered into the District that exceed $100,000 or 200 or more separate retail sales delivered into the District.
Marketplace Facilitator Information: Marketplace facilitators are required to collect sales tax on behalf of their marketplace sellers.

State: A marketplace facilitator is a person that provides a marketplace that lists, advertises, stores, or processes orders for retail sales subject to sales tax for sale by marketplace sellers, and directly or indirectly collects payment from a purchaser and remits payment to a marketplace seller regardless of whether the marketplace facilitator receives compensation or other consideration in exchange for its services.
Economic Nexus Rules: No
Marketplace Facilitator Information: No
CSP Program: Annually when sales tax liability is <= $200.00 during the preceding calendar year

State: Quarterly when sales and use tax liability is greater than or equal to $201 and less than or equal to $1200 per period
Monthly when sales and use tax liability is greater than or equal to $1201 per period

If the 20th falls on a weekend or holiday, the return is due the next business day. You must file a return, even if you did not make any sales or no sales and use tax is due. Otherwise, you will receive a delinquency notice.
